## Gift Ideas For Older Parents And Grandparents (Jess’ Dad Again, FTW)

By [Les Bunge](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/author/les-bunge)

Dec 9

Welcome to our official guide for great ideas for older parents and grandparents! Les (Jess’ dad) is, of course, here to give you his thoughtful and cool ideas, but we also included some great ideas for moms and grandmas. So please enjoy and don’t forget to [check out even more ideas on our shop page](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/shop?category=for-parents)![🙂](https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f642.svg)

## For The Men (From Les)

Well, it’s that time of year again. The holidays are once again upon us. As wonderful as this time of year is, it can also be daunting. Hopefully, my gift guides can relieve some of the pressure. Lately, I’ve been noticing all of these cute items for baby girls. You pick up one outfit and almost immediately find another even cuter. I buy that one and then stumble upon another. Geez, I never once imagined the effect that a granddaughter would have on me. Mr.Tough guy is now the softest one on earth. Yikes!  Give me strength.

Anyway, let’s see if I can remember some of the cool stuff I came across this past year. I have to start by going through my belongings. When I see something unique, I usually buy it. Ha.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_104-Small.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2BEIa7VIvRiDdmz9H5pXegEQ)

[CROSLEY RADIO CD PLAYER](https://rstyle.me/%2BEIa7VIvRiDdmz9H5pXegEQ)
[$80](https://rstyle.me/%2BEIa7VIvRiDdmz9H5pXegEQ)

When I was living in NYC, I ran across a guy who restored vintage radios and installed Bluetooth in them. I bought one and have been totally happy with it. It’s nice to listen to my music on a vintage piece. I wondered if any retailers sold anything similar. Well, Crosley has exactly that. Well, not quite exactly. It’s [a new retro radio](https://rstyle.me/%2BEIa7VIvRiDdmz9H5pXegEQ). It’s beautiful and sounds great. (I bought one for my cabin.)

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_110-Small.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2Bc-kuVqEXvBRgJI2Up4ZOjw)

[CUCKOO CLOCK](https://rstyle.me/%2Bc-kuVqEXvBRgJI2Up4ZOjw)
[$179](https://rstyle.me/%2Bc-kuVqEXvBRgJI2Up4ZOjw)

Something else I bought for [my cabin](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/the-diy-cabin-with-affordable-cabin-decor) was a [vintage cuckoo clock](https://rstyle.me/%2BnGtDG72LgF2u8LMo-N6MBw). I remember one in my grandma’s house. It not only makes me feel good, but I can tell what time it is without looking at the clock. Just count the cuckoos. Ha, who knew? (Grandma never informed me). They are easily found online. Most clock repair shops have a few. Make sure it has a silencing lever so as not to keep you awake. I leave it on so I know when to get the hell out of bed.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_101.jpg)](https://rstyle.me/%2BFEk4VxMUJRH8aBbK2KQi7w)

[GIARDINO NATURAL TABLECLOTH](https://rstyle.me/%2BFEk4VxMUJRH8aBbK2KQi7w)
[$158](https://rstyle.me/%2BFEk4VxMUJRH8aBbK2KQi7w)

Every once in a while, I’ll stumble into a shop that seems to carry a different kind of product than run-of-the-mill gift shops. One such shop exists in Healdsburg, CA. called [Forager](https://foragerhealdsburg.com/). I walked in because of a [Giardino natural tablecloth](https://rstyle.me/%2BGLdAZ-HioZaXk7WRVseh5Q) with a fantastic grapevine pattern. It wasn’t inexpensive, but I just couldn’t resist. It will be perfect in my outdoor kitchen when I am feeding the masses. (I do that sometimes.) It’s always easier for me to cook for others than for myself. Plus, I then have someone to talk to. Ha!

![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_10.jpg)

This tablecloth will enhance our already beautiful tablescape.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_96.png)](https://batlestudio.com/collections/classic-objects/products/medium-quill)

[QUILL PENCIL](https://batlestudio.com/collections/classic-objects/products/medium-quill)
[$65](https://batlestudio.com/collections/classic-objects/products/medium-quill)

At that store, I also picked up [a graphite pencil](https://batlestudio.com/collections/classic-objects/products/medium-quill) in the shape of an old writing quill. It’s just beautiful sitting on my desk. I have always preferred pencils to pens. It’s probably because I make a lot of mistakes. The pencil is titled Graphite Object by Battle Studio. I went on their website, and they have many more designs. It is probably not worth the money except that it is just damn cool. I like being damn cool.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_99.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2B_mZJDuR51XcHnr4Uu2XL5g)

[SPARE TIRE BRAKE LIGHT](https://rstyle.me/%2B_mZJDuR51XcHnr4Uu2XL5g)
[$59](https://rstyle.me/%2B_mZJDuR51XcHnr4Uu2XL5g)

In my travels, I was behind a Ford Bronco like my own, but when he hit the brakes, [a large circular brake light](https://rstyle.me/%2B_mZJDuR51XcHnr4Uu2XL5g) lit up behind his spare tire. Wow, again kinda cool. So I went online and found one. It was very affordable, and it was plug-and-play. It was installed in about ten minutes. Easy peasy!

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_98.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2B9zKtkVKgYGwX_R8ad9HplQ)

[BULLET ANTENNA](https://rstyle.me/%2B9zKtkVKgYGwX_R8ad9HplQ)
[$30](https://rstyle.me/%2B9zKtkVKgYGwX_R8ad9HplQ)

Another thing for my vehicle that my son turned me on to was [a car antenna](https://rstyle.me/%2B9zKtkVKgYGwX_R8ad9HplQ) that is in the shape of a large caliber gun shell. Now I am not a gun guy, but again kinda cool for a red-blooded American man. I can hear Jess rolling her eyes as I type, ha. Plus, you don’t have to worry about it in a car wash. It’s only about 4 inches high. So it’s not a statement, it’s just slick.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_97.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2BEngebQk9eI7n0alWVkdpDg)

[SHARK MESSMASTER VACUUM](https://rstyle.me/%2BEngebQk9eI7n0alWVkdpDg)
[$164](https://rstyle.me/%2BEngebQk9eI7n0alWVkdpDg)

With all the work I do around here, I make a lot of sawdust. It’s a mess! I’ve had all sorts of shop vacs to clean up. They’re great, but usually have a filter to clean when you empty them. Eventually, the filter has to be replaced. Well, I discovered the [Shark Messmaster](https://rstyle.me/%2BEngebQk9eI7n0alWVkdpDg). There is no filter to clean or replace. Everything goes into a built-in container that you just lift out and empty like the trash. Piece of cake. You can put a plastic trash bag in it so you never have to clean it, just throw the bag away, yeah, impressive. Love making my life easier.

![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_5.jpg)
![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_2.jpg)

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_101.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2BLiglEy9weVXp2Pd5JsABBQ)

[APPLE PEELER/CORER](https://rstyle.me/%2BLiglEy9weVXp2Pd5JsABBQ)
[$100](https://rstyle.me/%2BLiglEy9weVXp2Pd5JsABBQ)

I have a few apple trees on my property, and all of a sudden, they decided to produce. Yikes, what am I gonna do with apples? I can only bake so many pies. I have a bow and arrow, but my kids refuse to put an apple on their head for their William Tell father. Then I thought of apple sauce. I can give it to my neighbors for the holidays. Yay! So I needed an [apple peeler corer](https://rstyle.me/%2B-0H_b31I_niE33EqLK5rJA). Found one at Williams-Sonoma. Seeing as I live in Sonoma, how could I pass it up? Worked great and am on my second batch. I’m gonna need more neighbors.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_103-Small.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2Bg3T9PHaer3_reZ6s_Jf5pg)

[OVAL CERAMIC HONING ROD](https://rstyle.me/%2Bg3T9PHaer3_reZ6s_Jf5pg)
[$120](https://rstyle.me/%2Bg3T9PHaer3_reZ6s_Jf5pg)

As long as I’m in the kitchen, let me tell you about [my new knife honer](https://rstyle.me/%2Bg3T9PHaer3_reZ6s_Jf5pg). Steelport is a knife company in Portland, Oregon. I know they are passionate about their knives. My metal knife honer seems to have disappeared. Funny, so have my car keys. I should’ve shaken down the grandkids before they left the premises after Thanksgiving. Anyway, in my search for a new honer, I wandered into a beautiful wooden handled ceramic honer from Steelport. I have never seen a ceramic one. It is so beautiful, I don’t even care if it works or not. I know Steelport knives and cannot imagine it won’t. BTW, their knives are extraordinary too.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_95.png)](https://www.brickofchicago.com/buy/2026-desktop-calendar)

[CHICAGO DESK CALENDAR](https://www.brickofchicago.com/buy/2026-desktop-calendar)
[$19](https://www.brickofchicago.com/buy/2026-desktop-calendar)

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_94.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2BGIkVVksGqQYv7x7XEhCuww)

[CALIFORNIA DESK CALENDAR](https://rstyle.me/%2BGIkVVksGqQYv7x7XEhCuww)
[$15](https://rstyle.me/%2BGIkVVksGqQYv7x7XEhCuww)

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_93.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2B0bHQ_6IliU0Qp7GfKMRcog)

[NEW YORK DESK CALENDAR](https://rstyle.me/%2B0bHQ_6IliU0Qp7GfKMRcog)
[$35](https://rstyle.me/%2B0bHQ_6IliU0Qp7GfKMRcog)

This time of year, I start looking for calendars, so I know where and when I’m gonna be next year. There are all sorts of cool ones out there. I’m a Chicago native, and I found one called the [Brick of Chicago](https://www.brickofchicago.com/buy/2026-desktop-calendar). It pictures different brick buildings throughout the city. All of which I am familiar with. It’s a desktop one, so I will also buy a wall calendar with big numbers. Ha. Whatever your guy, dad, or husband is into, I am sure a calendar exists for that.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_100.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2BpQKJeUzXq7GinPqWmxuG0g)

[VINTAGE TOOTHPICK HOLDER](https://rstyle.me/%2BpQKJeUzXq7GinPqWmxuG0g)
[$23](https://rstyle.me/%2BpQKJeUzXq7GinPqWmxuG0g)

I have a tendency to always have a toothpick in my pocket. I guess I got into the habit when I was a teenager, thinking I was a badass dude. Anyway, I found a silver toothpick box to keep on my table. It looks great and keeps my badass toothpicks close at hand. I love it.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_102-Small.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2BPnQG1g91ycbdYDDz9IYkhw)

[VINTAGE MILKSHAKE MIXER](https://rstyle.me/%2BPnQG1g91ycbdYDDz9IYkhw)
[$60](https://rstyle.me/%2BPnQG1g91ycbdYDDz9IYkhw)

Lately, I’ve been craving malted milk shakes (oh oh, reverting to my youth again). So, of course, I bought [a single-stem mixer](https://rstyle.me/%2BPnQG1g91ycbdYDDz9IYkhw). I found a vintage one on [Etsy](https://rstyle.me/%2BImZQEtvspFkHs34fRwPMqQ). Most evenings, I will have a strawberry malt for my after-dinner drink.  I remember my father and I walking to Kainz Dairy in Chicago when I was a kid. He would get a banana malt, and I would get a strawberry one. Seems like yesterday. My nightly routine has become one of my small pleasures in life. I need all of those that I can get. It takes a little practice to get the malt perfect, but once you’ve got it, it’s like riding a bike. You never forget.

![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_11.jpg)

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_106-Small.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2B30aDOekQcYFhxQP9h4r8bA)

As you guys know, I travel the planet extensively. I have found that European men seem to dress much less casually than American men. I like that. The holiday season is when I can get away with dressing up whenever I leave the house. I don’t mean a suit, but a nice shirt or a nice T-shirt and [a sport coat](https://rstyle.me/%2BjSkAD05NWLS7FsWdrLiMbQ) do wonders for my mental state. When it gets a little chilly up here, I will even break out the fedora. I may not be GQ material, but I can fake it pretty well, ha! It’s amazing what a nicely starched white cotton dress shirt can do for one’s appearance. When buying a white dress shirt, just put your hand inside of it and see if you can see your hand through it. If you can, the cotton fabric is not quality enough. You don’t want to be able to see your hand through it. Anyway, that’s my system. It seems to work. I always find [the best quality white shirts](https://rstyle.me/%2B30aDOekQcYFhxQP9h4r8bA) at Nordstrom. The Brooks Brothers brand is also quite nice. They closed their stores, but [their shirts](https://rstyle.me/%2Bqwk47q2W3S21kiWpimDj4w) are still carried by Macy’s and some other department stores.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_108-Small.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2BvPXWl_RkppcRIlL8D61HlA)

[CHESS PIECE CUFFLINKS](https://rstyle.me/%2BboZV3ru2UwGS3yQsCD_Ydw)
[$100](https://rstyle.me/%2BboZV3ru2UwGS3yQsCD_Ydw)

I am a sucker for French cuffs and cufflinks. I have a huge assortment from solid gold Texas stars to [silver airplanes](https://rstyle.me/%2BvPXWl_RkppcRIlL8D61HlA). Whatever your guy is into, I guarantee you can find cuff links to match. It’s impressive when you give someone a French cuff shirt with the cuff links already added. It’s just plain old cool. I know I’m old school, but hell, I love it.

![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_3.jpg)
![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_4.jpg)

I wore my tuxedo to see [Masquerade](https://masqueradenyc.com/), the audience immersion version of Phantom of the Opera, in New York City last month. What an evening! Now that would be one hell of a gift. It is in the top 5 experiences of my entire life. There’s a statement.

![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_1.jpg)
![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_6.jpg)
![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_7.jpg)

I hope you found my suggestions helpful. I really enjoy sharing them with you. I’ve been on this planet for a while and have come to know there are no “Big Deals” in life. It’s just a bunch of little pleasures that, if you’re lucky, you can string them together and have a happy life. Remember, quality time is the best gift you can give anyone. It is a gift that always comes back to you. So why not cough that one up? Of course, in an emergency, there is always [the jean jacket](https://rstyle.me/%2BV3Wn8VjeaPPWzaZLn9Cjkw). Ha! I realize that most of these gifts are for men (the gender I am guilty of), but don’t worry, up next Emily has great ideas for the women in your life, the stronger of the two sexes. I wish all of you a happy and love-filled holiday season.

Gratefully,

Jessica Bunge’s Dad

## For The Ladies

Ok, so Suz (who is turning 80 this year!!) and my mom are both really hard to buy “things” for. Remember when Suz wrote the first boomer gift guide called [“No more scarves!”](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/the-ultimate-non-boring-gift-guide-for-boomer-moms-and-grandmothers) Message received. We always give plane tickets to come see us, and I’ll throw together a photo book. But it’s true that more stuff tends not to be what they want, and I get it (same). I asked her for a list to share, but it was mostly gift cards or experiences. But there are a few categories that seem to be still popular for IRL presents.

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_113-copy.png)](https://rstyle.me/%2B3pkfO-cOqWSyDQFEAz9FmA)

[AURA FRAME](https://rstyle.me/%2B3pkfO-cOqWSyDQFEAz9FmA)
[$179](https://rstyle.me/%2B3pkfO-cOqWSyDQFEAz9FmA)

[![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_2025_Boomers_112.jpg)](https://www.journiapp.com/)

[PHOTO BOOK](https://www.journiapp.com/)
[$20](https://www.journiapp.com/)

I know that boomer-aged parents (or grandparents) typically don’t want more stuff or clutter, but it’s clear they want to be surrounded by memories of those they love. A digital frame, like the [Aura Frame](https://rstyle.me/%2B3pkfO-cOqWSyDQFEAz9FmA), is still a fantastic gift, and if you want to make the world’s easiest photo book, go for [Journi](https://www.journiapp.com/) (seriously, SO FAST thanks to AI that automatically populates it chronologically).

![](https://stylebyemilyhenderson.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Emily-Henderson_Gift-Guide_Boomers_Moms_Dads_Men_Women_9.jpg)

Really, what they seem to want is gift cards so they can shop, time with family/friends, and new experiences. Plane tickets to visit (or money towards them) are always appreciated. I buy a lot of [Airbnb gift cards](https://www.airbnb.com/giftcards), which they can use towards places to stay AND experiences in town. If I can project into the future a bit, I know that all I would want is time and experiences with my kids or grandkids, or sentimental, thoughtful gifts that remind me of my life with them. Thanks, Les, for your insight per usual. The internet sure does love you.

# The 45 Best Gifts for Grandparents

Updated April 8, 2026

1 Deal

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2025/11/GIFTS-GRANDPARENTS-2048px-0617-2x1-1.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2:1&auto=webp)![Gift ideas for grandparents arranged on a light gray surface, including a digital photo frame, a pair of dark blue slip-on slippers, a teal tin of loose leaf tea, a blue tin of shortbread cookies, a set of stacked books with a pair of binoculars resting on top, a teacup and saucer with a small plate of cookies, an open palette of watercolor paints, a custom photo puzzle box showing a young girl, and a smart bird feeder shaped like a small house, with a red toy bird sitting on the dish of birdseed.]()

Michael Murtaugh//NYT Wirecutter

[![Samantha Schoech](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2023/04/Samantha-Shoech.jpg?auto=webp&quality=60&crop=1:1&width=50)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/authors/samantha-schoech/)

By [Samantha Schoech](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/authors/samantha-schoech/)

Samantha Schoech is a writer focusing on gifts. She spends her time finding things that combine quality, beauty, usefulness, and delight.

The greatest gift for most grandparents may be spending time with their grandkids, but that doesn’t mean they wouldn’t also appreciate a little something. After all, they do have interests beyond their immediate family.

We’ve scoured our guides and polled our colleagues to find the very best things to delight grandparents, including a [deluxe bird-watching station](#bird-watcher) for nature lovers, [master classes](#lifelong-learning) for the eternally curious, comfort for [gardeners](#easy-gardening), and [snacks](#scrumptious-snacking) for the gourmands. And if the grandkids really are their favorite thing, we have gifts for that, too.

Check out dozens of other gift possibilities for your grandma and grandpa in our many gift guides, including the [best retirement gifts](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/best-retirement-gifts/), [gifts for frequent travelers](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/best-gifts-for-travelers/), [gifts under $50](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/best-gifts-under-50/), the [best gifts for Mom](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/best-gifts-for-mom/), and the [best gifts for Dad](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/best-gifts-for-dad/).

## Toasty toes

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2026/01/GIFTS-GRANDPARENTS-2048px-verloop.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:2048&auto=webp)![The Verloop House Socks in a bright color block pattern of pink, green, blue, yellow, orange, and red.]()

Verloop

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/78170/Verloop-Kaleidoscope-Knit-House-Socks_20260330-205811_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/78170/232520/4/251178?merchant=Verloop)

## [Verloop Kaleidoscope Knit House Socks](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/78170/232520/4/251178?merchant=Verloop "Verloop Kaleidoscope Knit House Socks")

Keep tootsies warm with these maximalist slipper-socks with grippy soles. The fleece-lined socks are plush, machine-washable, and just plain fun to wear.

[$36 from Verloop](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/78170/232520/4/251178?merchant=Verloop)

It’s a fact that as people age, circulation slows and extremities get colder. That’s the bad news. The good news is that you get to wear lots of cozy things to remedy the issue. Help your beloved grandparents stay toasty with the epically snug and fun-to-wear [Verloop House Socks](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/78170/232520/4/251179/?merchant=Verloop). They have a thick fleece lining that provides cushion and warmth, grippy bottoms to prevent slipping, and bright, bold colors and patterns for flair and pizzazz. The high-ankle length stays up without binding or squeezing the leg, and when the socks collect dust and other schmutz from the floor, they can be machine-washed and dried. Writer Samantha Schoech wears hers to bed every night during the colder months and stays in them when she gets up in the morning.

## Regular reading

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2024/09/booksubscriptions-2048px-bookofthemonth.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:1536&auto=webp)![A person holding a pile of colorful books.]()

Book of the Month

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/40268/Book-of-the-Month_20250418-045157_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/40268/180469/4/251191?merchant=Book%20of%20the%20Month)

## [Book of the Month](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/40268/180469/4/251191?merchant=Book%20of%20the%20Month "Book of the Month")

The nearly century-old original subscription service prompts members to choose from a curated list of six or seven popular new releases each month.

[Buy from Book of the Month](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/40268/180469/4/251191?merchant=Book%20of%20the%20Month)

If the grandparent in your life is a reader, this book subscription service is a no-brainer. Each month, [Book of the Month](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/40268/180469/4/251180/?merchant=Book%20of%20the%20Month) sends members a choice of about seven titles in different genres — usually romance, mystery, sci-fi, narrative nonfiction, literary, and historical fiction. Once they pick, the book is sent directly to them. If they don’t see something they’re interested in, they can skip a month or choose from an extensive list of past picks. Prepaid gift subscriptions are available for three, six, and 12 months. If choosing a title via an online portal is more than your grandparents want to deal with, consider some of our other [favorite book subscription services](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/book-subscription-box/), which automatically send a hand-picked title each month without any back-and-forth.

## Scent from heaven

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/64357/Creekside-Farms-Dried-French-Herb-Wreath_20250418-052918_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/64357/217708/4/251192?merchant=Farm%20Fresh%20To%20You)

## [Creekside Farms Dried French Herb Wreath](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/64357/217708/4/251192?merchant=Farm%20Fresh%20To%20You "Creekside Farms Dried French Herb Wreath")

A study in muted greens with gentle pops of color, this 16-inch-diameter, aromatic wreath features herbs like lavender, sage, and bay leaves grown on a family-owned California farm.

[$60 from Farm Fresh To You](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/64357/217708/4/251192?merchant=Farm%20Fresh%20To%20You)

[$75 from Creekside Farms](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/64357/217707/4/251192?merchant=Creekside%20Farms)

The [Creekside Farms Dried French Herb Wreath](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/64357/217708/4/251181/?merchant=Farm%20Fresh%20To%20You) weaves together dried herbs and flowers into a heavenly scented 16-inch ring worthy of a French farmhouse. It’s made in California with a mix of lavender, thyme, bay, and sage, and it’s potent enough to scent a room and delicious enough to inspire an appetite (though this gift is strictly for display purposes). The made-to-order wreath arrives securely attached inside a structured cardboard box for safekeeping. Its vibrant colors become more muted within a week, but the herbs keep their shape and structure for months. Creekside Farms has [other lovely wreaths](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70026/215999/4/251182/?merchant=Creekside%20Farms), as well as a [seasonal subscription service](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80194/236925/4/251183/?merchant=Creekside%20Farms).

## Audio of yesteryear

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2025/10/BEST-PORTABLE-CD-PLAYERS_2048px_SYITREN-R300_HERO-1.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:1365&auto=webp)![The Syitren R300 portable CD player, closed.]()

Hannah Schwob for NYT Wirecutter

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/71850/Syitren-R300_20250602-214856_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/71850/219791/4/251193?merchant=Amazon)

## [Syitren R300](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/71850/219791/4/251193?merchant=Amazon "Syitren R300")

This portable CD player has a built-in transmitter that’s compatible with Bluetooth speakers and headphones, and its rechargeable battery eliminates the need to stock up on AAs.

[$95 from Amazon](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/71850/219791/4/251193?merchant=Amazon)

Grandparents who never switched from CDs to digital music are in luck: Apparently, CDs are making a comeback. The compact [Syitren R300](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/71850/219791/4/251184/?merchant=Amazon) CD player is our top pick in [our new portable CD player guide](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/best-portable-cd-player/) because it has user-friendly controls (just five buttons, one knob, and a power switch), runs on a nine-hour rechargeable battery, and produces excellent sound. In our testing, it paired over Bluetooth without fuss or frustration. This one small unit — about the size of a sandwich box — might just replace a bookshelf’s worth of audio equipment from the ’90s.

## History class

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2026/04/GIFTS-GRANDPARENTS-2048px-historymail.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:2048&auto=webp)![An individual’s hands hold a line drawing titled "D-DAY DRESS PLATOON LEADER." The sketch shows a soldier surrounded by labels for gear like a carbine, canteen, and gas mask.]()

History by Mail

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/80195/History-by-Mail_20260406-165518_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80195/236926/4/251194?merchant=History%20by%20Mail)

## [History by Mail](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80195/236926/4/251194?merchant=History%20by%20Mail "History by Mail")

Each month, this subscription service sends a collection of replicas of historical documents along with contextual information on a different event or era in American history.

[Buy from History by Mail](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80195/236926/4/251194?merchant=History%20by%20Mail)

This beautifully analog subscription will send your history-buff grandparent a collection of replicas of historical documents that provide an in-depth look at important events and eras in America’s past. One month might focus on D-Day and include copies of Eisenhower’s Order of the Day letter and original sketches of soldiers’ dress, along with documents providing historical context for each replica. The themes change every four months and cover topics such as inventions, American wars, and foreign diplomatic relations. [History by Mail](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80195/236926/4/251185/?merchant=History%20by%20Mail) offers one-, six-, 12-, and 24-month subscriptions with optional add-ons for subjects like national parks and urban legends for about $50 each.

## Toast upgrade

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2026/03/GIFTS-MOMS-2048px-marmalade.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:1365&auto=webp)![A closeup of the Marmalade Grove “Party Of Six” Gift Set.]()

Marmalade Grove

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/79125/Marmalade-Grove--Party-Of-Six--Gift-Set_20260217-230751_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/79125/236083/4/251195?merchant=Amazon)

## [Marmalade Grove Party Of Six Gift Set](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/79125/236083/4/251195?merchant=Amazon "Marmalade Grove Party Of Six Gift Set")

The tangerines, Pixie mandarins, Meyer lemons, Ruby Valencia oranges, and Cara Cara oranges used in this marmalade are grown and tree-ripened in Ojai, California.

[$70 from Amazon](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/79125/236083/4/251195?merchant=Amazon)

All the citrus used in this small-batch, handmade marmalade is grown on the Marmalade Grove farm in Ojai, California, where it's made. The beauty of the [Marmalade Grove Party of Six Gift Set](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/79125/236083/4/251186/?merchant=Amazon) is the ability to taste the distinct fruit flavors of each inventive blend, such as the honey sweetness of Pixie mandarins in the Sunrise Marmalade and the sweet-savory of the Oroblanco grapefruit and sweet agave spread. Each one pairs terrifically with morning toast, but your giftee can also dollop them atop ice cream or spread them on crackers with goat cheese.

## News from the big day

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/80196/The-New-York-Times-Front-Page-Reprint_20260406-170136_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80196/236928/4/251196?merchant=The%20New%20York%20Times)

## [New York Times Front Page Reprint](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80196/236928/4/251196?merchant=The%20New%20York%20Times "New York Times Front Page Reprint")

These reproductions of the front page, going all the way back to 1851, are printed on 100% cotton, acid-free archival paper. They come in three sizes, with or without a frame.

[$60 from The New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80196/236928/4/251196?merchant=The%20New%20York%20Times)

What was happening on the day your grandparent was born, or got married, or graduated high school? [New York Times Front Page Reprints](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80196/236928/4/251187/?merchant=The%20New%20York%20Times) go all the way back to 1851 and show the most important news of the day exactly as it appeared then (older editions are in black and white, while newer ones include color photographs). The broadsides come in three sizes and can be ordered framed or unframed. You can also get them in [puzzle form](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/80197/236929/4/251188/?merchant=The%20New%20York%20Times).

## Cool health monitor

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2025/09/BEST-IPHONE-SMARTWATCHES-9160.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:1365&auto=webp)![An Apple Watch SE with a geometric patterned background sitting on a pink surface.]()

Michael Hession/NYT Wirecutter

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/74079/Apple-Watch-SE--3rd-generation-_20250915-204813_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/74079/225192/4/251230?merchant=Amazon)

## [Apple Watch SE (3rd generation)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/74079/225192/4/251230?merchant=Amazon "Apple Watch SE (3rd generation)")

This stylish emergency-contact system can detect falls, car crashes, and certain types of cardiac events, as well as send help automatically.

[$219 from Amazon](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/74079/225192/4/251230?merchant=Amazon)

[$219 from Walmart](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/74079/224928/4/251230?merchant=Walmart)

[$249 from Apple](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/74079/224674/4/251230?merchant=Apple)

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/56017/Fitbit-Inspire-3_20250723-001620_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/56017/190832/4/251197?merchant=Amazon)

## [Fitbit Inspire 3](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/56017/190832/4/251197?merchant=Amazon "Fitbit Inspire 3")

This easy-to-use tracker is comfortable to wear all day and provides accurate measurements, without too much clutter on the home screen.

[$80 from Amazon](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/56017/190832/4/251197?merchant=Amazon)

[$80 from Walmart](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/56017/190834/4/251197?merchant=Walmart)

[$80 from Best Buy](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/56017/191622/4/251197?merchant=Best%20Buy)

Most grandparents aren’t hankering for the newest tech gadget, but even if they eschew digital in favor of all things analog, the [Apple Watch SE](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/74079/225192/4/229715/?merchant=Amazon) could become an important companion. While the sleep and fitness trackers may or may not be of interest, the heart-rate notifications, fall and crash detection, and automatic emergency-calling system could prove helpful indeed. Although the watch is no substitute for medical care and can’t detect heart attacks, it can alert wearers of irregular heartbeats or unusual temperatures and automatically call emergency services if it detects a hard fall. Even if they never use it to read an email or make a call, it might provide a little peace of mind. Meanwhile, [our favorite fitness tracker](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/the-best-fitness-trackers/) doesn’t have all the bells and whistles of an Apple Watch, but it does track steps, sleep, calories burned, and heart rate, all on its face. For those who want more data, the [Fitbit Inspire 3](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/56017/190832/4/251189/?merchant=Amazon) can work with its app to track weight, glucose levels, stress management, and skin temperature, plus water and food intake.

## Spot of tea

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2023/09/tea-2048px-3052.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:1365&auto=webp)![A can of the Fortnum & Mason Royal Blend tea, to the left of a small white plate with a small clump of the loose-leaf tea on it.]()

Connie Park/NYT Wirecutter

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/47604/Fortnum---Mason-Royal-Blend-Tea_20250418-050235_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/47604/175959/4/251227?merchant=Amazon)

## [Fortnum & Mason Royal Blend Tea](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/47604/175959/4/251227?merchant=Amazon "Fortnum & Mason Royal Blend Tea")

This 100-plus-year-old blend is an extraordinary version of an ordinary black tea.

[$18 from Amazon](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/47604/175959/4/251227?merchant=Amazon)

This excellent black tea from the legendary London department store elevates the daily cuppa into something truly special. [Fortnum & Mason Royal Blend](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/47604/175959/4/229713/?merchant=Amazon) was created for King Edward VII in 1902 and combines Assam and pekoe leaves for a smooth, slightly sweet flavor. It’s perfect with a dash of cream and sugar, and even better with some Scottish shortbread. Writer and tea aficionado Alexander Aciman chose it as one of the [best teas we’ve tasted](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/best-teas/) (he also appreciates the excellent tins and uses them for desktop storage once the tea is gone). The Royal Blend comes loose or in plastic-free, fully compostable tea bags.

## Give them lemons

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2025/09/GIFTS-GRANDPARENTS-2048px-meyerlemontree.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:2048&auto=webp)![A small Meyer lemon tree with green leaves and a few yellow lemons sits inside a white basket next to a white armchair.]()

Fast Growing Trees

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/55206/Fast-Growing-Trees-Meyer-Lemon-Tree_20251126-153739_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/55206/201708/4/251224?merchant=Fast%20Growing%20Trees)

## [Fast Growing Trees Meyer Lemon Tree](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/55206/201708/4/251224?merchant=Fast%20Growing%20Trees "Fast Growing Trees Meyer Lemon Tree")

This pretty, mail-order citrus tree, which can grow indoors or outdoors in warmer climates, produces fragrant blossoms and eventually fruit in the right conditions.

[$66 from Fast Growing Trees](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/55206/201708/4/251224?merchant=Fast%20Growing%20Trees)

Depending on the [plant hardiness zone](https://planthardiness.ars.usda.gov/) where your grandparents live, this [Meyer Lemon Tree](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/55206/201708/4/229714/?merchant=Fast%20Growing%20Trees) can grow indoors in a planter or outdoors. It provides sweet-smelling blossoms, and with tons of sunlight, citrus fertilizer, and well-draining soil, it can eventually bear fruit (Samantha’s outdoor tree took five years to produce lemons but is now offering a dozen perfect yellow orbs at a time). Due to agricultural restrictions on shipping citrus, the tree is not available for shipment to certain states (check the sidebar on the product page), and Fast Growing Trees does not send it to cold-weather states in the winter.

## Excellent vision

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2025/09/GIFTS-GRANDPARENTS-2048px-travelbinoculars.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:1365&auto=webp)![A pair of dark green Pentax AD 8x25 WP Binoculars is sitting on a weathered wooden stump, with a black case next to it.]()

Kyle Fitzgerald/NYT Wirecutter

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/26173/Pentax-AD-8x25-WP_20251031-201717_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/26173/138009/4/251207?merchant=Amazon)

## [Pentax AD 8x25 WP](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/26173/138009/4/251207?merchant=Amazon "Pentax AD 8x25 WP")

Compact binoculars aren’t the best for birding, but this pair combines pocket-sized convenience with enough magnification for a person to actually identify things.

[$146 from Amazon](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/26173/138009/4/251207?merchant=Amazon)

[$149 from Walmart](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/26173/184472/4/251207?merchant=Walmart)

Whether your grandparents like to identify birds and other critters or just keep tabs on the neighbors, these lightweight, compact binoculars will help. [Our pick for travelers](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/the-best-binoculars/), the [Pentax AD 8x25 WP](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/26173/138009/4/229716/?merchant=Amazon) binoculars, weigh just under 11 ounces, have 8x magnification, and show bright, clear images. The 21mm eye cups are comfortable, and the focus is easy, making long days of spying … er, birding easy and squint-free.

## For the bird…lovers

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2025/04/grandparentsgifts-loon.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:2048&auto=webp)![A person wears the green Bird Collective Loons of North America Sweatshirt in green with a bird print.]()

Bird Collective

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/70322/Bird-Collective-Loons-of-North-America-Sweatshirt_20250418-054044_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70322/216552/4/251229?merchant=Bird%20Collective)

## [Bird Collective Loons of North America Sweatshirt](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70322/216552/4/251229?merchant=Bird%20Collective "Bird Collective Loons of North America Sweatshirt")

This comfy, loose-fitting, cotton-polyester sweatshirt cutely depicts the work of artist Jeffrey Burke.

[$65 from Bird Collective](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70322/216552/4/251229?merchant=Bird%20Collective)

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/70306/The-Backyard-Bird-Chronicles_20250418-054041_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70306/216526/4/251232?merchant=Amazon)

## [The Backyard Bird Chronicles](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70306/216526/4/251232?merchant=Amazon "The Backyard Bird Chronicles")

In this best-selling nonfiction book, novelist Amy Tan turns her narrative talents to the birding world and debuts her aptitude for painting, too.

[$18 from Amazon](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70306/216526/4/251232?merchant=Amazon)

[$20 from Walmart](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70306/216785/4/251232?merchant=Walmart)

Also sure to resonate: [Amy Tan’s newest book](https://www.nytimes.com/2024/07/10/realestate/amy-tan-the-backyard-bird-chronicles.html), *[The Backyard Bird Chronicles](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70306/216526/4/215816/?merchant=Amazon)*, in which the novelist pulls from her own journals and sketchbooks of observing birds in her Northern California backyard. The nonfiction work is a surprisingly gripping chronicle of her foray into the world of birding — “seeing and being seen” — with sharp observations about how humans interact with the natural world, alongside Tan’s own beautiful watercolors.

Self-dubbed “[geriatric starlet](https://www.nytimes.com/2024/03/01/fashion/iris-apfel-dead.html)” Iris Apfel — the late, beloved interior designer — found fame in her 80s and 90s when the Metropolitan Museum of Art put on a last-minute exhibition of her wild closet contents as a replacement for a cancelled exhibit. The iconoclastic, silver-haired New York society matron quickly became a worldwide phenomenon for her daring personal style: exaggerated, colorful glasses, huge bangles, and a red-and-green feathered evening coat. And *[Iris Apfel: Colorful: A Treasure Trove of Inspiration, Influences, and Ideas](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/70326/216556/4/215820/?merchant=Amazon)* puts her fabric inspirations, philosophy, and journey of self-expression on display in 300 personal photos, replete with a bright green-and-yellow ribbon bookmark. Any free-spirited or creative granny or grandpa is sure to appreciate her empowered, bold thinking and its stylistic manifestations. As she once said: “When you don’t dress like everybody else, you don’t have to think like everybody else.”

## [Storyworth](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/41942/167103/4/251215?merchant=Storyworth "Storyworth")

This gift-worthy subscription service sends weekly prompts to the recipient so that they can write their own stories, which are then compiled along with personal photos or art into a hardcover book.

[$99 from Storyworth](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/41942/167103/4/251215?merchant=Storyworth)

Prompts like “What’s the furthest you’ve ever traveled?” “Do you have any regrets in your life?” or “What is one of your fondest childhood memories?” are designed to get the memories and stories flowing. The answers are emailed to you as they come in and, at the end of the year, bound into a quality hardcover book, preserving a grandparent’s legacy and lots of family stories for future generations. “Watching my dad’s book come together filled me with the kind of joy that’s usually reserved for a parent witnessing their child take the stage for the first time. And it’s a gift that helped him achieve a true dream: writing a book,” says Katie.

The archive also features classic telecasts and audio recordings dating back to 1935, including a spellbinding Callas in her only broadcast at the Met, in a 1956 performance of Donizetti’s [*Lucia di Lammermoor*](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/61443/200771/4/183741/?merchant=The%20Metropolitan%20Opera). Photos of grandkids are always a great gift, and that’s especially true when grandparents live far away. We highly recommend the [Aura Carver](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/53619/226684/4/161924/?merchant=Wirecutter%20Store), one of the [best digital frames](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/best-digital-photo-frame/) we’ve tried, to upload unlimited photos for loved ones. It has a large, bright screen for easy viewing and an intuitive interface, and the software makes setting up the frame as a gift especially simple. You can pair the device to your account (via email or by scanning a QR code on the box) and invite family members to contribute photos, all before the recipient unwraps their gift.

## A personal weather station

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2025/11/BEST-HOME-WEATHER-STATIONS-WeatherStations-Acurite-5.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=2048:1365&auto=webp)![An AcuRite Iris (5-in-1) outdoor weather sensor, featuring a wind vane and anemometer, is mounted on a post.]()

Kyle Fitzgerald/NYT Wirecutter

[![](https://d34mvw1if3ud0g.cloudfront.net/76246/AcuRite-Iris--5-in-1--Weather-Station-with-Direct-to-Wi-Fi-Display_20251106-215103_full.jpeg)](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/76246/229062/4/251217?merchant=Amazon)

## [AcuRite Iris (5-in-1) Weather Station with Direct-to-Wi-Fi Display](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/76246/229062/4/251217?merchant=Amazon "AcuRite Iris (5-in-1) Weather Station with Direct-to-Wi-Fi Display")

This all-in-one unit is easy to set up and measures five basic weather conditions — temperature, humidity, wind, rain, and pressure.

[$193 from Amazon](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/76246/229062/4/251217?merchant=Amazon)

[$193 from Walmart](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/76246/229142/4/251217?merchant=Walmart)

For grandparents who live somewhere [with a microclimate](https://www.worldatlas.com/articles/the-microclimates-of-san-francisco.html) or in a rural area where professionally monitored weather stations are scarce — or for those who are simply weather geeks — a personal weather station is an excellent choice. The easy-to-set-up [AcuRite Iris (5-in-1) Weather Station with Direct-to-Wi-Fi Display](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/76246/229062/4/245331/?merchant=Amazon) is [our favorite budget pick from testing](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/best-home-weather-stations/). With the myAcuRite app ([Android](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/76253/229137/4/245332/?merchant=Google%20Play), [iOS](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/out/link/76253/229068/4/245332/?merchant=Apple%20App%20Store)), you can create alerts for several variables and access historical charts and tables dating back 31 days. You can also export that data to a CSV file. Your weather-minded grandparent will love collecting big-picture trend data on specifics such as rainfall totals, temperature, humidity, and wind speed and direction. Earn extra points if you remember to ask them about the weather later.

## Meet your guide

![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2023/04/Samantha-Shoech.jpg?auto=webp&quality=60&crop=1:1&width=100)

Samantha Schoech

What I Cover

I discover, vet, and write about gifts of all kinds from my office in San Francisco. I am particularly drawn to all things shiny or bookish, but I try not to impose my taste more than is strictly necessary.

## Mentioned above

* [Help the soon-to-be retired person in your life enjoy their newfound freedom with gifts that encourage adventure, learning, and creativity.

  The 35 Best Retirement Gifts](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/best-retirement-gifts/)
* [Book subscriptions are a great way to discover new books and ensure that you or your loved one always have something good to read.

  7 Book Subscription Services We Love](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/book-subscription-box/)
* [The Syitren R300 is our favorite portable CD player because it offers a great mix of performance and convenience for a reasonable price.

  The Best Portable CD Player](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/best-portable-cd-player/)
* [After putting more than 50 wrist-worn fitness trackers through their paces — on the move and at rest — we have three to recommend.

  The Best Fitness Trackers](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/the-best-fitness-trackers/)
* [From iced herbal teas to strong black brews, here are the best teas according to Wirecutter’s obsessive staff.

  The Best Teas We’ve Tasted](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/best-teas/)
* [The Athlon Optics Midas ED are the best binoculars we’ve tested in almost a decade, thanks to crystal-clear optics that rival those found on professional pairs.

  The Best Binoculars for Birds, Nature, and the Outdoors](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/reviews/the-best-binoculars/)

## Further reading

* [![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2024/10/giftsfor8yrolds-2048px-8294-3x2-1.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=3:2&auto=webp)![An assortment of our favorite gifts for 8 year olds, including a music box, speed cube, and origami book.]()](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/best-toys-gifts-for-8-year-olds/)

  ### [Our Very Favorite Gifts for Creative 8-Year-Olds](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/best-toys-gifts-for-8-year-olds/)

  by Ellen Lee and Wirecutter Staff

  Many 8-year-olds are starting to discover their passions, from sports to STEM activities to the arts.
* [![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2026/01/GIFTS-KIDS-VALENTINES9555-3x2-1.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=3:2&auto=webp)![Several different products recommended in our Best Valentine’s Day Gifts for Kids.]()](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/valentines-day-gifts-for-kids/)

  ### [The Best Valentine’s Day Gifts for the Kids You Love](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/valentines-day-gifts-for-kids/)

  by Ellen Lee, Rachel Hurn, and Wirecutter Staff

  Whether you’re looking for one special gift or a project to complete together, we’ve gathered ideas to help spread the love this Valentine’s Day.
* [![](https://cdn.thewirecutter.com/wp-content/media/2025/11/GIFTS-EVERYONE-ON-YOUR-LIST-2048px-0961-3x2-1.jpg?width=150&quality=20&crop=3:2&auto=webp)![Various gifts arranged on a red-pink background. From left to right, the items include a pair of green wool slippers, a shiny cobalt blue ceramic vase shaped like a pouring vessel, a small gold table lamp with a vertical light bar, a jar of Luxardo Maraschino Cherries, a dark green plastic watering can, a small, pink, blob-shaped night light or novelty item, a cocktail glass, a clear glass cylinder filled with multicolored pom-poms, a Funko Pop figurine of a person standing on a smaller platform, a blue ceramic butter dish with a small knife, and a tall yellow pitcher filled with a bouquet of vibrant purple flowers.]()](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/best-christmas-gift-ideas/)

  ### [50 Unique Gifts for Everyone on Your List](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/best-christmas-gift-ideas/)

Other items include a children's plate decorated with a face and topped with raspberries and grapes for hair and eyes, two burrito-shaped Dice Poppers, a Throw Throw Burrito card game, and a yellow "Dance Party" button.]()](https://www.nytimes.com/wirecutter/gifts/best-family-gifts/)

# 10 Best Gifts for Older Parents

Finding the right gift for your parents isn’t about checking a box—it’s about choosing something that says *I see you, I care, and I want to make your days a little easier or brighter*. The most meaningful gifts for older parents support comfort, independence, and everyday well-being. As parents age, their priorities often shift, which is why the best gifts for aging parents focus less on “things” and more on quality of life. Whether it’s a practical item that simplifies daily routines or a thoughtful touch that brings warmth and reassurance, truly great gifts for elderly parents blend usefulness with heart.

Below are 10 thoughtful gift ideas for elderly parents that are meaningful, practical, and appreciated for any special occasion, at any time of year.

## 1. Large-Screen Digital Photo Frame

For parents who prefer simple, no-fuss technology, a preloaded WiFi digital frame is one of the best gifts for older parents. It keeps family close, offers daily joy, and requires almost no maintenance.

## 2. Automatic Jar Opener or Kitchen Aid Tool

Struggling with lids or small kitchen tasks is common. A jar opener is a truly useful gift for older parents, offering convenience and independence.

## 3. Cozy Heated Throw or Blanket

A heated throw provides warmth and comfort—making it a thoughtful option when considering gifts for senior parents who appreciate practical coziness.

## 4. Wireless Key or Item Finder

If your parents often misplace essentials, this can be one of the most good gifts for elderly parents—reducing stress and keeping daily life easier.

## 5. Smart Speaker or Voice-Activated Assistant

Simple voice commands for reminders, music, or calls make these devices among the top gifts for older parents, especially those wanting a little help with daily tasks.

## 6. Foot or Leg Massager / Gentle Wellness Gadget

Wellness-focused tools are great gift ideas for older parents, offering relaxation and improved circulation.

## 7. Comfortable Slip-On Shoes or Supportive Slippers

Easy on/off shoes or slippers are classic great gifts for older parents, supporting mobility, comfort, and safety.

## 8. Subscription Box or Monthly Treat Delivery

Subscription boxes make wonderful gift ideas for aging parents because the joy continues month after month. Snacks, puzzles, or cozy items work particularly well.

## 9. Large-Print Calendar, Daily Clock, or Memory Journal

For those with vision or memory challenges, these items are best gifts for elderly parents who value organization and simplicity.

## 10. A Shared Experience or Personalized Keepsake

Sometimes the most meaningful gift for older parents isn’t a product—it’s time together. Outings, shared meals, or personalized keepsakes create memories that last.

## Gift-Giving Tips for Elderly Parents

1. **Focus on usability:** Think large print, simple controls, and ergonomic design.

1. **Prioritize their needs:** Comfort, safety, mobility, and convenience matter most when choosing gifts for elderly parents.

1. **Add sentimental value:** Preloaded photos or handwritten notes make even simple gifts feel special.

1. **Consider budget & space:** Thoughtful gifts don’t need to be expensive or bulky.

1. **Set things up for them:** Especially important for tech-related gift ideas for elderly parents.

## Give the Gift of Giving

Elderly parents often find great joy in giving, not just receiving. While some may face physical or financial limitations, supporting their desire to share warmth, kindness, and love can be deeply meaningful. Helping them participate in giving—whether through small gestures, shared activities, or thoughtful contributions—can sometimes be the greatest gift we can offer them.

## For Elderly Parents Who Have Everything

If you’re searching for gifts for elderly parents who have everything, one of the most impactful gifts you can offer is independence—through compassionate, reliable help.

## Get Them a Reliable Caregiver in Time for the Holidays

The truth is, one of the best gifts for older parents is the ability to maintain independence at home. A caring caregiver who understands their needs—while still giving them space—can make an enormous difference in their daily comfort, safety, and happiness. # Great gift ideas for aging parents, grandparents

Jean Long Manteufel

For USA TODAY NETWORK-Wisconsin

Dec. 2, 2017, 8:49 a.m. CT

![](/gcdn/authoring/authoring-images/2026/06/02/USAT/90370586007-military-sisters-graduation-surprise-leaves-brother-in-tears-thumb-4.jpg?crop=1918,1078,x0,y0&width=400&height=225&format=pjpg&auto=webp)

**Dear Jean:**

I recently bought your book, “Transitions – stories of how to help Mom and Dad with their stuff.” Almost daily I have a constant anxiety about my stuff. I just finished the chapter “Shopping for Elderly Parents? We’ve got Answers” and wish you’d run it in the paper again — with a headline that will grab adult children’s/grandkids' attention. I get lovely gifts from my family, but those gifts are among the stuff that I’ll have to be sorting through, possibly getting rid of. — Signed, Too Much Already

**Dear Too Much:**

Here you go, along with some updates. The original question was: “What do we get Grandma and Grandpa for Christmas? They say they don’t want or need anything, yet we still want to show we care.”

I asked readers. Here are their responses. No one mentioned fancy coffee makers, electronics or general “stuff.”

Need a news break? [Check out the all new PLAY hub with puzzles, games and more!](https://play.usatoday.com/?itm_source=SPIKE&itm_campaign=PLAYSPIKE&itm_medium=ONSITE)![Jean Long Manteufel]()

A common suggestion was gift cards: Cards for groceries, hair salon/barber and for the pharmacy to help with co-pays.

One gent responded: “We still give my 95-year-young mother-in-law gift certificates to her favorite eating places. She loves to eat out, and even though we treat most of the time, she still likes to pull out a gift certificate and say, ‘Let me buy dinner tonight.’”

For the techie senior: A gift card for Nook books or iTunes gift cards for music downloads.

Another favorite is single-serving food, also soups, brownies and cookies. One gal said: “My parents always loved home-cooked meals, frozen and ready to microwave, especially as it got harder for Dad to see to cook and for Mom to stand long enough to cook.”

If they live in a community, consider gift certificates for additional meals and “banked” money for outside trips.

One friend said: “When my grandma was in the nursing home, I would massage her hands with her favorite lotion. It kept her hands nice, it was a way to connect through touching and it was a scent she enjoyed.”

More: Puzzles, stamps, box of birthday cards and thank you notes, easy crossword puzzle books, personalized calendars with birthdays and family events already noted.

Give your parents the gift of independence with the push-the-button-for-help units. It can make them feel safe and allow them to remain in their house.

Help around the house was a huge response. Offer your assistance to clean closets, cook a meal, spring-clean, shampoo carpets, clean windows, change furnace filters or balance their checkbook and pay bills.

Not surprisingly, the number one request was giving the gift of you.

Make a coupon book and say you will: call them every Tuesday morning, take them to Friday fish, do weekly shopping trips, take them to their Sunday church service, have a movie night at their home complete with DVDs and popcorn, play cards/games, or bring grandchildren to visit.

Then do it.

Share this with your kids and encourage them to give Grandma and Grandpa something they can really use and enjoy. This Christmas, give a gift from the heart.

*Jean Long Manteufel, senior move manager and CEO of Long’s Senior Transitions in Appleton, can be reached at 920-734-3260 or Jean@TransitionsWithJean.com Manteufel’s book, “Transitions: Stories of how to help Mom and Dad with their stuff”, can be found at Atlas Coffee Mill, 425 W. Water St., Appleton or at www.TransitionsWithJean.com*

# Top 10 Thoughtful Gifts for Parents or Grandparents in Senior Living

Share:

![Family celebration with flowers and gift for a senior](https://www.srgseniorliving.com/wp-content/uploads/bigstock-Daughter-Mom-And-Granny-166468400.jpg "Daughter, Mom And Granny")

As the holiday season approaches, finding the perfect gifts for seniors can feel challenging, especially when you want something that shows how much you care. The best gifts for seniors are those that bring comfort, spark joy, or create lasting memories during this special time of year and beyond.

If you’re wondering what good gifts are for seniors in your life this holiday season, this curated guide offers ten meaningful ideas. These gifts genuinely resonate with parents and grandparents in senior living communities.

## **1. Custom Photo Albums and Digital Frames**

There’s something irreplaceable about holding memories in your hands, especially during the holidays. Create a personalized photo album chronicling family milestones, past holiday celebrations, or everyday moments that matter. Include handwritten captions sharing what those memories mean to you.

*Note: For a modern twist, digital photo frames that rotate through pictures can be updated remotely by family members, ensuring fresh holiday photos and memories appear throughout the season.*

## **2. Heritage Recording Services**

Preserve your loved one’s stories for generations to come. Services like [StoryWorth](https://www.mystories.com/holiday-sale?utm_source=ppc_google&tr_ag_id=179456807259&tr_device=c&tr_account=169-553-4148&matchtype=e&gad_campaignid=22534630231&gclid=Cj0KCQiAosrJBhD0ARIsAHebCNqwvWIcRUl3LjowpKiByg6Wzk_cln-goQVNleIAOj-ZmJ6nLUdFBIwaAmPSEALw_wcB&keyword=&gbraid=0AAAAADwjG2m212iAZ23cUtUq4h_FbCORY&tr_camp_id=22534630231&tr_ad_group=storyworth&utm_campaign=mh_search_us_en_all_mystories_all_generic&gad_source=1&utm_medium=cpc&utm_term=storyworth&lng=en) send weekly prompts via email and compile responses into a beautiful keepsake book. These recordings become priceless treasures that capture their wisdom, humor, and life experiences in their own words.

## **3. Ultra-Soft Weighted Blankets**

As temperatures drop and cozy season arrives, comfort matters more than ever. High-quality weighted blankets can improve sleep quality and provide a soothing sense of security during long winter evenings. Choose breathable fabrics appropriate for the season and a weight that feels comforting without being overwhelming—typically between 5-15 pounds, depending on preference.

## **4. Technology That Connects**

A tablet pre-loaded with video calling apps and family photos makes staying in touch effortless. Take time to set it up completely, with large-icon shortcuts and simple instructions. Add subscriptions to streaming services so they can enjoy their favorite shows, movies, and music with ease.

## **5. Regular Visit Commitments**

Sometimes the best gifts for seniors aren’t things at all—they’re your time. Establish a standing weekly lunch date, a Sunday-afternoon visit, or a monthly outing. Consistency matters more than grand gestures, and these regular touchpoints become cherished routines that provide something to look forward to.

## **6. Subscription Services for Ongoing Joy**

Think beyond one-time holiday gifts. Subscriptions to flower deliveries, audiobook platforms, gourmet snack boxes, or monthly magazines related to their interests provide ongoing delight throughout the new year. Each delivery serves as a reminder that someone cares, extending the holiday spirit well into the months ahead.

## **7. Customized Family Tree Art**

Commission a beautifully designed family tree that celebrates their legacy. Many artists create custom pieces incorporating names, birthdates, and even miniature portraits. It’s a daily reminder of the family they’ve nurtured and the connections that endure across generations.

## **8. Spa-Quality Personal Care Items**

Elevate daily routines with luxurious touches: ultra-soft towels, gentle bath products, fragrance-free moisturizers, or an electric heating pad. These small luxuries transform ordinary moments into something special and demonstrate thoughtfulness toward daily comfort.

## **9. Professional Family Photography Sessions**

This holiday season, arrange for a professional photographer to capture current family portraits. These photos become increasingly precious over time and create wonderful opportunities for everyone to gather together during the holidays.

Many photographers also visit senior living communities to make the experience more convenient and comfortable. This is a perfect opportunity to capture multiple generations in one beautiful session.

## **10. Memory Quilts From Meaningful Fabrics**

Transform treasured clothing—a grandfather’s flannel shirts, a grandmother’s dresses, or vintage family linens—into a cozy quilt. This wearable memory provides physical warmth while honoring sentimental value, creating a gift that’s both practical and deeply personal.

## **Choosing the Right Gift**

When looking for gifts for seniors, consider their current lifestyle, physical comfort needs, and what brings them genuine joy. The most meaningful presents often combine practicality with sentiment—a cozy blanket paired with a photo album, or a commitment to regular visits alongside a tablet for staying connected between trips.

For those wondering “What are good gifts for seniors?”, remember that the gift itself matters less than the thought and love behind it. Senior living residents particularly appreciate items that help them stay connected to family, maintain their interests and hobbies, or simply add comfort to their daily routines.

The most treasured gifts for seniors aren’t necessarily the most expensive—they’re the ones that say, “I see you, I value you, and you matter deeply to me.” Whether you choose a heritage recording service, a standing lunch date, or a spa collection, your thoughtfulness will shine through.

## **Creating Meaningful Moments at SRG Communities**

At [SRG Senior Living](https://www.srgseniorliving.com/), we understand that the best gifts for seniors often complement the vibrant lifestyle our communities provide. With over 35 years of pioneering innovative senior living experiences, [our communities](https://www.srgseniorliving.com/communities/) create the perfect backdrop for the meaningful gifts you choose this holiday season.

Many of these gift ideas pair beautifully with life at an SRG community. Our communities offer [independent living](https://www.srgseniorliving.com/living-choices/independent-living/), [assisted living](https://www.srgseniorliving.com/living-choices/assisted-living/), and [memory care](https://www.srgseniorliving.com/living-choices/memory-care/) options, ensuring your loved ones have access to the support they need while maintaining the lifestyle they deserve.

When you’re selecting gifts this holiday season, consider how they enhance the person-centered wellness programs, creative pursuits, and social connections that are already part of daily life at SRG.

At every stage of life, feeling loved and remembered is the greatest gift of all, especially during the holidays. These ten ideas offer a starting point for showing your parents or grandparents just how much they mean to you, creating moments of joy and connection that extend far beyond the initial unwrapping.

Ready to learn more about how SRG communities enrich daily life for residents? [Explore our communities](https://www.srgseniorliving.com/communities/) to discover the perfect setting for your loved one’s next chapter.
